title: Untitled
research_problem: The ever-increasing number of research articles makes manual literature review cumbersome, time-consuming, and error-prone, creating a need for automated systems that can generate literature reviews from PDF files as input.
method_summary: Three approaches are implemented and compared: (1) Frequency-based approach using spaCy for word frequency calculation and sentence extraction, (2) Transformer-based approach using Simple T5 model fine-tuned on SciTLDR dataset, (3) LLM-based approach using GPT-3.5-TURBO-0125 with retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) using SciTLDR as knowledge base.
tasks: Automated literature review generation from PDF files; Scientific document summarization
datasets: SciTLDR dataset from Hugging Face (5,400 TLDRs from over 3,200 scientific papers)
claim_text: The LLM-based model (GPT-3.5-turbo) achieved the highest ROUGE-1 score of 0.364, outperforming both T5 (0.268) and spaCy (0.257)
limitations: The system is limited to English language scientific documents only | SciTLDR dataset may not cover all scientific domains, limiting applicability to other fields | The ROUGE scores are relatively low (highest ROUGE-1 is 0.364), indicating room for significant improvement
notes: The paper implements three complete end-to-end pipelines for literature review automation; A GUI tool was created allowing users to upload PDF files and automatically generate literature reviews; The LLM-based approach uses RAG with SciTLDR as the knowledge base; The system processes PDFs to extract abstract, introduction, and conclusion sections before summarization; User evaluation was performed with IoT-related research papers demonstrating practical application

title: Untitled
research_problem: The ever-increasing number of research articles makes manual literature review cumbersome, time-consuming, and error-prone, creating a need for automated systems that can generate literature reviews from PDF files as input.
method_summary: Three approaches are implemented and compared: (1) Frequency-based approach using spaCy for word frequency calculation and sentence extraction, (2) Transformer-based approach using Simple T5 model fine-tuned on SciTLDR dataset, (3) LLM-based approach using GPT-3.5-TURBO-0125 with retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) using SciTLDR as knowledge base.
tasks: Automated literature review generation from PDF files; Scientific document summarization

claim_text: All three NLP approaches (spaCy, T5, and GPT-3.5-TURBO) can produce satisfactory results in automating literature review generation
limitations: The system is limited to English language scientific documents only | SciTLDR dataset may not cover all scientific domains, limiting applicability to other fields | The ROUGE scores are relatively low (highest ROUGE-1 is 0.364), indicating room for significant improvement
notes: The paper implements three complete end-to-end pipelines for literature review automation; A GUI tool was created allowing users to upload PDF files and automatically generate literature reviews; The LLM-based approach uses RAG with SciTLDR as the knowledge base; The system processes PDFs to extract abstract, introduction, and conclusion sections before summarization; User evaluation was performed with IoT-related research papers demonstrating practical application

title: Untitled
research_problem: The ever-increasing number of research articles makes manual literature review cumbersome, time-consuming, and error-prone, creating a need for automated systems that can generate literature reviews from PDF files as input.

title: Untitled
research_problem: The ever-increasing number of research articles makes manual literature review cumbersome, time-consuming, and error-prone, creating a need for automated systems that can generate literature reviews from PDF files as input.
method_summary: Three approaches are implemented and compared: (1) Frequency-based approach using spaCy for word frequency calculation and sentence extraction, (2) Transformer-based approach using Simple T5 model fine-tuned on SciTLDR dataset, (3) LLM-based approach using GPT-3.5-TURBO-0125 with retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) using SciTLDR as knowledge base.
tasks: Automated literature review generation from PDF files; Scientific document summarization
datasets: SciTLDR dataset from Hugging Face (5,400 TLDRs from over 3,200 scientific papers)
claim_text: Large language models are superior to older NLP approaches (frequency-based and transformer-based) for automated literature review generation
limitations: The system is limited to English language scientific documents only | SciTLDR dataset may not cover all scientific domains, limiting applicability to other fields | The ROUGE scores are relatively low (highest ROUGE-1 is 0.364), indicating room for significant improvement
